prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) was performed using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method in the gas phase (charge 0, multiplicity 1). The Fukui indices were computed via a finite-difference scheme using Mulliken partial charges for the neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, mult 2) states. The headline value reported is the f⁺ (electrophilic attack) index for Fe (atom 11, 0-based index 10), which is also the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic site in the molecule. Fe f⁺ = 0.22885456, Fe f⁻ = 0.25938191, Fe f⁰ = 0.24411824, Fe dual descriptor = −0.030527354. All Cp-ring carbons (~0.01364 each for f⁺) and hydrogens (~0.06348 each for f⁺) show much smaller Fukui indices. The negative dual descriptor on Fe confirms it is the dominant nucleophilic center — consistent with the known electron-rich iron d-orbital character in ferrocene. Perfect Cp-ring symmetry in the Fukui values (both rings identical to numerical noise) provides a strong internal consistency check. Integrity status: trustworthy = true. Sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. No warnings were reported. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 ferrocene.xyz
